notes.text: Baseline panel. Blood draw on day 7 of cycle (early follicular). Ferritin already borderline low — doctor recommended iron supplementation. Feeling fatigued, especially around periods.

Mid-luteal draw (day 22). Progesterone elevated as expected for this phase. Ferritin dropped further despite 25mg iron. Switching to iron bisglycinate 36mg with vitamin C. TSH creeping up — monitoring.

Early follicular draw (day 5). Hemoglobin lowest yet — almost at lower reference limit. Iron saturation below range. Increased iron dose. TSH continues rising, fT4 dropping. GP ordered thyroid antibodies for next visit.

Day 10 (late follicular). Iron and hemoglobin finally improving! Two months of consistent high-dose iron bisglycinate working. But TSH still climbing — fT4 now near lower boundary. Endocrinology referral pending.

diagnoses.note: Family history: Hashimoto's thyroiditis (mother), osteoporosis (maternal grandmother). No other chronic conditions. Pending thyroid antibody testing (TPO, Tg).

diet.breakfast: Eggs with spinach, sourdough toast, berries
diet.lunch: Grilled chicken salad with quinoa and roasted vegetables
diet.dinner: Salmon or red meat with sweet potato and greens
diet.note: Higher protein focus (~120g/day). Red meat 2-3x/week for heme iron. Takes iron supplement away from coffee/tea. Green tea 1-2 cups/day.

exercise.note: Yoga 3x/week (vinyasa + yin). Running 2x/week (5-8 km easy pace). Light resistance training 2x/week since Nov 2025 for bone health. Rest days aligned with heavier period days. Daily 6000+ steps.

sleepRest.note: 22:30-05:30 typical schedule. Occasional insomnia during luteal phase (3-4 nights/month). Melatonin 0.5mg occasionally.

lightCircadian.note: Morning sunlight walk 15 min. Works from office with large windows. No shift work history.

stress.note: Work deadlines can be intense but manageable. Health anxiety around thyroid diagnosis and iron issues.

loveLife.note: Libido fluctuates with cycle — lowest during late luteal/early menstrual. Partner supportive.

environment.note: Lives in a quiet residential area of Prague. Building renovated 2018 — new windows, old structure.

interpretiveLens: Interested in functional medicine approach to thyroid and iron. Follows a Hashimoto's prevention strategy focused on optimizing ferritin to 50+ µg/L (functional range), not just above lab minimum. Concerned about thyroid trajectory given family history.
contextNotes: Proactive about health and prefers functional/optimal reference ranges over standard lab ranges. Particularly concerned about thyroid trajectory given family history of Hashimoto's diagnosis.

healthGoals.text: Build ferritin to 50+ µg/L and maintain stable iron status
Clarify thyroid status — determine if subclinical hypothyroidism or early Hashimoto's
Improve energy and reduce fatigue, especially premenstrual
Maintain vitamin D above 75 nmol/L year-round
Support bone density (family history of osteoporosis)

periods.notes: Normal flow, less fatigue than usual — iron helping?
Heavier than usual, 6 days. Extra fatigued.

assessments.label: Initial home assessment

rooms.name: Bedroom

rfMicrowave.meter: Safe Living Technologies Safe and Sound Pro II

rooms.note: Courtyard-facing — shielded from cell towers by building mass. Low RF. AC electric slightly elevated from unshielded wiring in renovated walls. Bed canopy added for backup since neighbor's WiFi is detectable.

rooms.name: Living Room

rfMicrowave.meter: Safe Living Technologies Safe and Sound Pro II

rooms.note: Street-facing windows — cell tower on building across the street (approx 80m). RF notably higher on this side. Multiple neighbor WiFi networks visible.

assessments.note: Self-measured with rented meters. Pre-1970 building, renovated 2018. Thick masonry walls provide good shielding on courtyard side. Street side has direct line-of-sight to cell tower. Considering shielding curtains for living room windows.
assessments.interpretation:
## Sleep room is the priority

- **Bedroom AC magnetic field** measured high — likely from external wiring or transformer adjacent to the room. Highest-leverage finding because it accumulates 8h/night of exposure.
  - **Mitigation:** Baubiologie measurement; sleeping head-position relocation; correction from the utility if persistent.
- **WiFi router** placement could be improved — 4–5 m distance from sleeping/working areas drops exposure ~16× via inverse-square. Alternative: hardwire ethernet + disable WiFi at night via timer.
- **Phone at bedside** — power off or airplane mode at night eliminates near-field exposure. Removing the phone from the room is even better.

Lower priority:
- Office RF baseline acceptable.
- Living-area dirty electricity moderate; consider Stetzer filters on heaviest-load circuits.

aiAnalysis.tip: Setup complete — II skin, Prague, CZ, Ott score 6/10
aiAnalysis.detail: Skin type II drives the burn-threshold + vit-D yield curves. Prague (~50°N) means winter sun is too low for vit-D synthesis Nov-Feb; cap session length aggressively in summer noon. Ott score 6/10 suggests room for evening light-burden reduction.

lightDevices.notes: Anchor device — calibrated against dminder app. Use full mode 5×/wk midday.
Bedside — used post-workout for recovery + on workdays as eye-channel anchor.

aiAnalysis.tip: Solid 25-min session, well within safe band
aiAnalysis.detail: 32% body fraction at UVI 5.8. Vit-D synthesis on track, 23% MED leaves room for one more session today if you want.

aiAnalysis.tip: Face-only exposure — circadian + eye good, vit-D minimal
aiAnalysis.detail: ~5% of skin exposed. Strong morning circadian + violet-eye signals at the eye, but vit-D synthesis stays low under 10% body fraction. If vit-D is a goal, a single mid-day arms-out session covers more ground than four face-only ones.

rooms.name: Bedroom
rooms.notes: Switched to amber bulbs for evening + blackout curtains installed mid-March.

aiAnalysis.tip: Warm bedroom light + evening use — good evening config
aiAnalysis.detail: 2700K amber bulbs + 1-3hr of post-sunset use. Melanopic load stays low, sleep onset uninterrupted.

rooms.name: Office
rooms.notes: Window-side desk; natural daylight 9am–4pm. Overhead LED only on cloudy days.

aiAnalysis.tip: Cool-white workspace, daytime-only — appropriate for office
aiAnalysis.detail: 5000K + 7h/day during productive hours feeds the daytime alertness signal. Window-adjacent is even better — natural light dominates 9am-4pm.

aiAnalysis.tip: 3 h/day — light-burden moderate
aiAnalysis.detail: Mixed/unknown lighting in a low-occupancy room is a low-impact lever. Focus on bedroom + workspace first.

aiAnalysis.tip: Blue blocker + minimal evening use — well managed
aiAnalysis.detail: laptop with active blue-light filter + <1hr after sunset. This is the ideal screen profile.

aiAnalysis.tip: Unblocked phone + 1-3hr after sunset
aiAnalysis.detail: Phone/tablet display peaks at ~440nm — same wavelength your circadian system reads as "morning sun". A schedule-based blue-blocker (Night Shift / f.lux) cuts the melatonin-suppression effect ~40% without changing your usage habits.

burdenAI.tip: Indoor light load is moderate — bedroom evening use is the main lever.
burdenAI.detail: Daytime exposure is good (window-side office, kitchen mixed). Evening bedroom is the weak spot: 1–3hr screen + warm-LED is ok for sleep but 1+ unblocked hr of phone reduces melatonin onset by ~30 min on average. Consider blue-blockers on phone after 9pm OR cutting evening phone use to <1hr.

lightAudits.label: Baseline — pre-bedroom upgrade
lightAudits.notes: Before swapping bedroom bulbs to amber + installing blackout curtains.

rooms.name: Office
rooms.notes: Window-side desk; natural daylight 9am–4pm. Overhead LED only on cloudy days.

aiAnalysis.tip: Cool-white bedroom overhead is the main melatonin suppressor.
aiAnalysis.detail: 4000K bedroom lighting at evening use breaks the sleep-onset signal. Pair this with 1–3hr unblocked phone time and you get measurable phase delay. Highest-leverage fix is bulb swap to <2700K + blackout for the sleep window.

lightAudits.label: After — amber bulbs + blackout
lightAudits.notes: Swapped bedroom to 2700K amber + blackout curtains for sleep window.

rooms.name: Bedroom
rooms.notes: Switched to amber bulbs for evening + blackout curtains installed mid-March.

rooms.name: Office
rooms.notes: Window-side desk; natural daylight 9am–4pm. Overhead LED only on cloudy days.

aiAnalysis.tip: Bedroom melatonin signal restored. Evening phone is now the next lever.
aiAnalysis.detail: Amber bedroom lighting + blackout brings the bedroom into the green band for melanopic-EDI. Remaining lever is evening phone use (1–3hr unblocked) — adding a screen-level blue-block schedule would close the loop.

aiAnalysis.tip: 320 lux — daytime-low for an office
aiAnalysis.detail: Reasonable but below the 500-lux office baseline. Adding window-side time or a daytime-bright lamp would help alertness.

aiAnalysis.tip: 18000 lux — strong daytime signal
aiAnalysis.detail: Plenty of light for the circadian pathway during productive hours.

aiAnalysis.tip: 6 lux — sleep-spec dark
aiAnalysis.detail: Below 50 lux is the ICNIRP-friendly sleep range. Melatonin uninhibited.

aiAnalysis.tip: 2700K — warm, evening-appropriate
aiAnalysis.detail: Below 3000K means low blue content. Good for sleep prep.

aiAnalysis.tip: 4900K — neutral, room-by-room call
aiAnalysis.detail: Mid-CCT works for daytime task lighting; in evening rooms it accelerates melatonin suppression.

aiAnalysis.tip: Flicker score 1 — mild
aiAnalysis.detail: Detected at 240 Hz. Below 200 Hz is correlated with eye strain + headache susceptibility in flicker-sensitive subjects (Wilkins 2018).

aiAnalysis.tip: Warm — incandescent-like
aiAnalysis.detail: Warm-spectrum lighting in evening rooms minimizes melanopic load.

lightMeasurements.notes: Mean 0.2 lux post-blackout, brief 1.4 peak from streetlight

aiAnalysis.tip: Sleep-spec ✓ mean 0.2 lux at the pillow
aiAnalysis.detail: Sleep darkness goal is <1 lux mean. Brief streetlight peaks (max 1.4 lux) are tolerable; constant exposure isn't.

channelMixAI.tip: Strong red-light + morning circadian; vitamin-D channel a bit light
channelMixAI.detail: Weekly mix shows good red-light therapy coverage and morning circadian signal. Vitamin D channel sits at ~70% of weekly target — one more 25-min midday outdoor session would close the gap. Near-infrared from solar tracks your outdoor time; cellular-repair channel is well covered. Mood and outdoor-eye channels good.

focusCard.text: Ferritin 42 µg/L (Jan 2026) is up from 24 in Sep — Iron Bisglycinate is doing real work over the 4-month run, but still under the functional 50+ goal you set. Hemoglobin recovered to 138 g/L from a Sep low of 126, and transferrin saturation tracks the same shape, so the iron-status arc is consistent. TSH and free T4 remain in range, but worth watching given the Hashimoto's family history — a paired thyroid-antibody panel (TPOAb + TgAb) would be the cleanest next step before adjusting the iron protocol.
